commands.review_summary:
The PR restarts a running managed gateway after a successful doctor git update, detects stale service-version metadata, activates running Windows update repairs, and removes Startup-folder fallbacks only after Scheduled Task running evidence.

PR surface: Source +91, Tests +242. Total +333 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the linked Windows report gives concrete commands and stale-module logs, and current main shows doctor update can finish without restarting the running gateway while update-mode service repair stages instead of activates. I did not run the native Windows repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review should keep this draft/protected PR open until native Windows/WSL proof and normal maintainer readiness are in place; no narrow automated code repair was identified.

target.title: fix(windows): repair doctor update fallback migration
target.body:
Summary:
- fixes the Windows doctor git-update path so a successful update restarts the running managed gateway instead of leaving the stale process on old dist chunks
- teaches doctor service repair to detect stale OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION metadata and only activate/migrate Windows services during update repair when the gateway was already running
- cleans up old Startup-folder fallback launchers only after Task Scheduler itself reports the Scheduled Task is running

Fixes #87156.

Verification:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/daemon/service-audit.ts src/commands/doctor-service-audit.test-helpers.ts src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/service-audit.ts src/commands/doctor-service-audit.test-helpers.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean after fixes

Real behavior proof:
- Behavior addressed: native Windows doctor/update repair no longer leaves a running Startup-folder fallback gateway on stale dist chunks, and migration cleanup avoids duplicate startup launchers.
- Real environment tested: focused mocked Windows service lifecycle tests locally; native Windows and WSL GitHub proof will be added before this draft is marked ready.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: the commands listed in Verification above.
- Evidence after fix: unit coverage now proves running Windows update repairs use the activating install path, stopped Windows update repairs stay staged, successful Scheduled Task activation/restart removes the Startup-folder launcher only when Task Scheduler reports the task running, and ambiguous old-listener evidence preserves the fallback.
- Observed result after fix: focused Vitest shards, targeted oxlint, oxfmt check, diff check, and autoreview passed.
- What was not tested: real elevated native Windows doctor/update migration and a real WSL distro path are pending remote/manual proof.
target.author: vincentkoc
